title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Evan Llewellyn meets with Mr. Parker Pyne to discuss the theft of the "Morning Star" diamond. Llewellyn denies involvement but expresses distress over the suspicion cast upon him by Sir George and Mr. Bohns. He explains that his recent prosperity, which he attributes to luck from horse racing, has drawn the attention of an undercover officer. During their conversation, Pyne suggests that Llewellyn's feelings regarding Lady Lymington or Mrs. Lestrange might be relevant. Pyne also asks Llewellyn to describe the physical features of everyone present on the yacht and inquires about a broken wine glass that was stepped on near the child, Ives, and Sir George Malory. Pyne requests a follow-up meeting in three days.

On Friday, Llewellyn visits Pyne’s office, where Pyne reveals that the Amalfi group has been arrested. Pyne explains that the group—consisting of a father, a son, and a daughter-in-law—used a sophisticated deception to steal the diamond. Maria Amalfi, posing as a sixteen-year-old girl, used play-doh to facilitate the crime. The real diamonds were stuck to the bottom of a plate with gum, while a fake diamond was passed through the group. To hide the evidence, Ivan Amalfi threw the fake diamond onto the floor, breaking a wine glass and stepping on the shards and the fake stone to make them indistinguishable. Pyne identifies Maria as the prime suspect because her youthful appearance and use of play-doh matched his criminal classifications. Llewellyn expresses his gratitude and departs for Lady Lymington's apartment.
